metadata.description: Hybrid cloud and on-premise deployment cost models for HELIOS v4.0

strategy_1_edge_plus_cloud.name: Edge Processing + Cloud Storage
strategy_1_edge_plus_cloud.description: On-premise agents for local processing, cloud for centralized storage and analytics
strategy_1_edge_plus_cloud.use_case: Organizations with strict data residency requirements who need cloud scalability

strategy_2_dev_prod_split.name: Development On-Premise, Production Cloud
strategy_2_dev_prod_split.description: Development and testing fleet on-premise, production fleet in cloud for auto-scaling
strategy_2_dev_prod_split.use_case: Startups growing rapidly, want to keep dev costs low while cloud handles growth

strategy_3_primary_backup.name: Primary Cloud, Backup On-Premise
strategy_3_primary_backup.description: Main workload in cloud for reliability, on-premise as disaster recovery
strategy_3_primary_backup.use_case: Mission-critical applications needing guaranteed DR capability

strategy_4_geographic_split.name: Geographic Distribution
strategy_4_geographic_split.description: Cloud agents in multiple regions, on-premise for compliance-restricted jurisdictions
strategy_4_geographic_split.use_case: Global enterprises with localization and sovereignty requirements

comparison_to_pure.verdict: Best for organizations needing local processing with cloud reporting

fleet_16_strategy2_dev_prod_split.name: Development On-Premise (8 agents) + Production Cloud (8 agents)

configuration.fleet_distribution: 50/50 split optimizes costs for growth scenarios

on_premise_costs.minimal_configuration: Single server setup for dev team

comparison_to_pure.verdict: Optimal for startups: minimal dev infrastructure, cloud-based production

growth_trajectory.year2: 32 agents (8 dev, 24 prod) - add cloud capacity
growth_trajectory.year3: 64 agents (8 dev, 56 prod) - cloud scales automatically
growth_trajectory.migration_path: As workload grows, transition more to cloud and eventually retire on-premise dev servers

fleet_24_strategy3_primary_backup.name: Primary Cloud (18 agents) + Backup On-Premise (6 agents)

comparison_to_pure.verdict: Best for mission-critical apps needing guaranteed DR capability

dr_cost_justification.notes: DR system justified if downtime cost exceeds $50K/hour

fleet_100_strategy4_geographic_split.name: Geographic Distribution (60 agents cloud + 40 on-premise)

configuration.compliance_requirements: GDPR for EU, data sovereignty for APAC

comparison_to_pure.verdict: Essential for global enterprises with compliance requirements

fleet_100_strategy4_geographic_split.compliance_benefit: Avoids €10K+ GDPR fines per incident; enables local data residency; satisfies sovereignty laws

hybrid_cost_curves.analysis: How hybrid costs scale across fleet sizes

findings.point_1: Small hybrid (8 agents) costs more than pure cloud due to on-premise overhead
findings.point_2: Medium hybrid (16-24 agents) becomes competitive as workloads distribute
findings.point_3: Large hybrid (100 agents) shows 35-40% savings vs pure cloud with on-premise benefits
findings.cross_over_point: ~20 agents where hybrid cost = pure cloud cost with better risk profile

from_pure_on_premise_to_hybrid.phase1: Establish cloud infrastructure for new workloads
from_pure_on_premise_to_hybrid.phase2: Gradually migrate non-critical services to cloud
from_pure_on_premise_to_hybrid.phase3: Decommission on-premise redundancy as confidence grows
from_pure_on_premise_to_hybrid.cost: Transition costs and duplicate infrastructure during migration period

from_pure_cloud_to_hybrid.phase2: Pilot edge agents in limited deployment
from_pure_cloud_to_hybrid.phase3: Expand on-premise footprint based on ROI
from_pure_cloud_to_hybrid.cost: On-premise infrastructure procurement, but reduces cloud egress costs

when_to_choose_hybrid: High data transfer costs in pure cloud scenario
Reduce latency for geographically distributed users
when_to_avoid_hybrid: Fleet size under 16 agents (overhead too high)
Limited IT ops staff (requires more expertise)
Geographic footprint fully within single cloud region
